task_completion.description: Select the correct patient record for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GNU Health window or tab displaying the patient record for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Document the occupational chemical exposure using the correct ICD-10 code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A conditions, evaluations, or occupational health screen showing the ICD-10 code 'Z57' (or a Z57.x variant) added to the patient's record.

task_completion.description: Record a clinical evaluation including systolic BP, diastolic BP, and heart rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation form with values entered into the fields for Systolic pressure, Diastolic pressure, and Heart rate.

task_completion.description: Order a biological monitoring lab panel with at least three laboratory orders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab request or laboratory orders screen showing at least 3 distinct tests (e.g., CBC, hepatic, renal function) requested for the patient.

task_completion.description: Document a new patient lifestyle record reflecting risk factors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Lifestyle' tab or form within the patient's record showing newly entered data regarding risk factors or habits.

task_completion.description: Schedule the next annual surveillance appointment for 335-395 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduling screen or calendar entry showing an appointment booked for John Zenon approximately one year (335-395 days) in the future.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client being used, rather than terminal windows executing database commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the actual application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into proper GNU Health input fields, dropdowns, and tabs, not just typed into a text editor or a mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the records in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of saved records, confirmation messages, or the use of 'Save'/'New' buttons in the GNU Health interface, rather than just filling out forms and abandoning them.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. The required ICD-10 code for occupational exposure is Z57 (or a Z57.x subcode). The agent must order a minimum of 3 lab tests. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 335 and 395 days from the current date. Note: Ignore any discrepancies regarding the patient's occupation or industry, as the database lists him as an 'Architect' and lacks an industry field; do not penalize the agent for ignoring the 'process technician' detail.
